title: Pagination: Focus & Announcement After a Page Change
summary: WAI-ARIA APG / NN-g / screen-reader-practice pass grounding backlog #059: when the user moves to a new page, where does focus land and what is announced? The load-bearing finding is that neither half is net-new vocabulary — both are already-owned intents the pagination block composes. Focus is the focus-after-view-swap question owned by Autofocus-on-Activation (landing: heading|target|preserve|auto, default heading; the APG answer), which Navigation already composes; the WebAIM thread shows no industry consensus, so it stays a configurable dimension. Announcement is the shipped clause-joined polite-status pattern (role=status + aria-atomic, W3C ARIA22 / WCAG 4.1.3) owned by Live Region Status — always announce "Page 2 of 10", a fixed mechanic, not a toggle. The platform offers no native focus/announce after a non-reload page change, so the only build is thin renderer wiring — zero new intents, zero protocols.
